description: Role-scoped rule proposals raised at the start of the iteration, before any actions are drafted. Lead is the role's authority — each proposal is accepted on the spot for this role.

rules.description: Role-scoped rule proposals. On a fresh iteration with no inherited root rules, propose 5–7 starting rules. When prior iterations have already promoted root rules for this role, propose only what is genuinely additive — smaller sets are normal.

proposal.description: The rule wording — the final form you'd enact.

alternatives.description: 1–3 other viable approaches you considered — genuinely different choices the user could pick instead, not rephrasings of the same proposal.

examples.title: Frontend lead, fresh iteration with no inherited root rules

rules.proposal: All UI primitives live in apps/web/src/components/ui/ as one .tsx file per component, lowercase-hyphenated (e.g. button.tsx, dialog.tsx).
alternatives: Primitives grouped by category folder (forms/, layout/, feedback/) under apps/web/src/components/ui/.
Primitives co-located with their first consumer page until reused twice.

rules.proposal: React components are named in PascalCase and exported as default; one component per file.
alternatives: Components named PascalCase, exported as named exports only (no default).
Multiple related components per file when they share private state; default-export the entry component.

rules.proposal: Page-level data fetching uses React Query hooks defined in apps/web/src/lib/queries/<feature>.ts and consumed by pages via useQuery.
alternatives: Data fetching co-located with the page component file.
All queries centralised in apps/web/src/lib/api.ts as a single namespace.

rules.proposal: Tailwind utility classes are written directly in JSX; class-variance-authority is used only when a primitive carries 3+ visual variants.
alternatives: All variants expressed via cva from the start, even for two-state primitives.
Inline utility strings only; no cva, variant logic via conditional class-name strings.

rules.proposal: Routes are declared in apps/web/src/App.tsx; nested layouts use the React Router <Outlet /> pattern.
alternatives: Routes split into apps/web/src/routes/index.tsx as a flat declaration list.
File-based routing via a convention scanner under apps/web/src/pages/.

examples.title: Backend lead, later iteration where root rules already cover most areas

rules.proposal: All Fastify route plugins are registered with a versioned prefix /v1 to support future API versioning without breaking existing clients.
alternatives: Versioning via Accept header rather than URL prefix.
Single unversioned namespace; defer versioning until the first breaking change.

examples.title: Lead with nothing additive to propose (rare)
